subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Sharp, sustained focus on the research question throughout the investigation
Clear articulation of sub-objectives that align tightly with the central theme
Effective communication of topic and purpose in all major sections
weaknesses: Methodological gaps: promised index of dispersion and sampling design details are not fully executed or justified
Limited critical reflection on source reliability (e.g. Wikipedia, real-estate websites)
Omission of key methodological specifics (scale calibration, mapping error)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation consistently communicates a sharply defined topic and research question and maintains this focus from title to conclusion, meriting placement in the top band (5â6). However, incomplete execution of some methods (missing dispersion results) and insufficient methodological detail prevent a perfect score. A mark of 5 accurately reflects this strong but not flawless performance.

strengths: Wide-ranging and generally accurate subject knowledge of urban geography and demographic trends
Accurate and consistent use of key geographical terminology and conceptual models
Effective integration of case-specific data (maps, graphs) to support factual understanding
weaknesses: Minor factual inaccuracies (e.g. mis-scaled population pyramid)
Reliance on a few non-academic definitions without scholarly citation
Occasional loose use of labels such as âurban dynamicsâ
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates excellent command of subject content and uses terminology consistently, placing it in the top band (5â6). Small inaccuracies and reliance on popular sources prevent the highest mark. A score of 5 reflects a very strong but not flawless use of knowledge and understanding.

strengths: Data application is consistently relevant and clearly tied to the research question
Analysis goes beyond description, linking trends to theoretical constructs like gentrification
Argument is generally coherent and focused, with evidence marshalled effectively
weaknesses: Some promised datasets (visitor time-series, dispersion statistics) are absent, undermining completeness
Causal assertions (e.g. tourismâs direct effect on fertility) are stated rather than empirically tested
Discussion occasionally lapses into description and unsupported generalisations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research application is strong and analysis is mostly supported, key data gaps and occasional untested assumptions moderate performance. These characteristics align with the 7â9 band descriptor for Critical Thinking. An 8 reflects the clear relevance and coherence of the argument, balanced against noted analytical weaknesses.

strengths: Comprehensive structure with all required sections (abstract through bibliography)
Headings, subheadings and a detailed table of contents guide the reader effectively
Consistent font, page numbering and inclusion of figures support readability
weaknesses: Duplicated figure numbers and truncated legends interrupt visual flow
Pixelated web images and occasional mis-alignment of graphics disrupt layout
Minor organisational slip-ups in figure commentary
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fulfils IB conventions and supports understanding at the top of the 3â4 band, but persistent minor layout errors prevent a perfect score. A mark of 4 appropriately rewards a clearly appropriate structure and generally correct layout, despite small flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning: The majority of the selected data sets (long-term population series, housing price trends, spatial mapping of attractions) are relevant and applied appropriately to address the research question.  However, several promised data (visitor time-series, completed dispersion statistics) are absent, and some numerical inconsistencies in migration figures undermine full credibility.  Consequently the application is âgood with clear relevanceâ rather than consistently excellent.

A mixed-methods strategy (historical demographic data, primary mapping of tourist resources, statistical measures such as dispersion indices, and qualitative observation) is well explained and mostly appropriate.  However, some stated techniques (e.g., index of dispersion) are not fully executed or the results are omitted, and reliability of certain secondary sources (Wikipedia, real-estate websites) is not critically justified.  These gaps reduce completeness slightly, placing the work at the lower end of the top band.

The essay displays wide-ranging and generally accurate knowledge of urban geography, tourism impacts and Polish demographic trends.  Case-specific information is detailed, and maps/graphs strengthen factual command.  Minor inaccuracies (e.g., mis-scaled population pyramid) and reliance on non-academic definitions (Collins Dictionary, Wikipedia) temper the otherwise strong grasp of subject matter, resulting in a solid but not flawless performance within the 5-6 band.

Page numbering, consistent font and inclusion of maps/figures suggest good attention to layout.  However, several figure numbers are duplicated (two âFigure 20â), some legends are truncated, and pasted web images occasionally appear pixelated or mis-aligned, interrupting visual flow.  These issues indicate that layout considerations are applied but not flawlessly, so a score at the lower end of the 3-4 band is appropriate.

Geographical terminology (e.g., âgentrificationâ, âindex of dispersionâ, âprimary/secondary attractionsâ) is employed accurately and with consistency across sections.  Conceptual models such as the âcycle of tourism-induced depopulationâ are integrated into the argument.  Nevertheless, a few terms are lifted from popular sources without scholarly citation, and some labels (e.g., âurban dynamicsâ) are used rather loosely.  These issues limit attainment to the mid-high level of the top band.

The student states a sharply defined topic â the impact of tourism-oriented urban development on population decline in Sopot â and maintains this focus consistently from title through conclusion. Each section of the study (introduction, data presentation, discussion and conclusion) clearly links back to the central theme, demonstrating an assured sense of purpose and direction. Occasional digressions into background description (e.g., lengthy restaurant examples) do not detract materially from the overall clarity; hence the work fulfils the descriptor for the top band.

The narrative is generally coherent: evidence from maps, graphs and literature is marshalled to argue that tourism drives up housing costs and accelerates depopulation.  Nonetheless, sections lapse into description (e.g., extended restaurant vignettes) and occasional first-person commentary disrupts academic tone.  Logical progression is sometimes weakened by unsupported generalisations, so the discussion is effective but with noticeable inconsistencies, matching the mid-band descriptor.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâtrends are identified and linked to theoretical constructs such as gentrification and urban life-cycle models.  Yet causal relationships (e.g., direct linkage between tourism growth and fertility decline) are asserted more than tested, and missing statistical verification weakens depth of evaluation.  The conclusion synthesises strands but rests on partly unsubstantiated assumptions.  This places the work solidly in the âadequate to goodâ 4-6 band, nearer the lower end.

The essay follows IB EE conventions: abstract, introduction, methodology, data presentation, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, and lists of figures/tables.  Headings and subheadings guide the reader and support comprehension.  Minor organisational issues (repetition of numbering, some misplaced figure commentary) are present but do not impede understanding; therefore the structure meets the top band for Presentation.

The research question is explicitly worded, appropriately narrow (âTo what extent does the prioritization of development of tourist attractions in Sopot impact population decline and urban dynamics?â) and is reiterated throughout the essay.  Sub-objectives are logically broken down and guide data collection and analysis.  The alignment between the research question, aims, theoretical background and concluding synthesis shows an effective and sustained focus, fulfilling the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: Including the word count at the start of the report demonstrates full compliance with IB formatting requirements. Well done.
Index of dispersion is listed as a method in Table 2 but its results do not appear in the analysis; ensure all promised techniques are fully executed and presented.
Figure 9âs long-term trend lacks discussion of data uncertainty or potential source inconsistencies post-2019; address data validity and possible anomalies.
Noise levels in Figure 24 should be compared to WHO or EU guidelines; benchmarking against standards would strengthen health impact evaluation.
Relying on a generic tourism definition without citing a peer-reviewed source limits subject knowledge; incorporate academic definitions to strengthen theoretical grounding.
Population pyramid (Figure 13) uses unrealistic scales (millions) for a small city; correct axis units to reflect actual population numbers.
Citing Wikipedia for urban social geography undermines academic rigor; replace with a scholarly source to improve source credibility.
Figure 2 is descriptive but does not analyze how these demographic factors specifically affect Sopot; link the model directly to local data for stronger evaluation.
The dot-map effectively shows attraction locations but lacks an embedded legend for color codes; include a clear map key for reader interpretation.
Migration chart categories (âallâ, âinternalâ) are not defined in the text or caption; clarify each categoryâs scope and meaning.
The four aims are well articulated and directly aligned with the research question, providing a clear roadmap for data collection and analysis.
The conclusion suggests strategies but lacks reflection on study limitations; include a critical self-evaluation to demonstrate deeper engagement.
The title is clearly stated and placed appropriately at the beginning, ensuring immediate clarity of the investigationâs focus.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, guiding the reader effectively through the structure of the essay.
The research question is clearly stated, focused on a specific issue, and sustained throughout the paper, demonstrating strong alignment with the investigationâs aims.
The methodology is introduced as mixed-methods but lacks detail on sampling design, data collection protocols, and assessment of source reliability; expand on these aspects for rigor.
Justification of the chosen area relies on personal observation; strengthen with external literature or comparative case justification to validate its selection.
The conceptual cycle in Figure 3 is clear but remains theoretical; apply and test this model using your mapped and statistical data to demonstrate empirical relevance.
The mapping technique is described generally but omits details on scale calibration, positional accuracy and mapping error; include methodological specifics for transparency.
The 21st-century decline graph would benefit from confidence intervals or treatment of statistical outliers to deepen analytical rigor.
Caption date (â19.11.2024â) conflicts with accessed date (â29.11.2024â); ensure consistency between your data collection dates and figure captions.
Map in Figure 16 lacks a legend explaining colored dots and the red boundary; include a legend and compass for full clarity.
Property offers shown in Figure 26 represent a small, non-random sample; discuss how these examples reflect broader market trends or supplement with aggregated statistics.
Figure 17 omits a legend for primary attraction symbols; incorporate a color key to guide interpretation of mapped features.
The restaurant vignettes in Figure 20 are descriptive but not linked quantitatively to depopulation trends; integrate spatial analysis with socio-economic data for depth.
The quotation on NOâ and PM pollution trails off without completion; ensure full citation and integrate with local air-quality data.
The housing-price series in Figure 25 lacks explanation of how price metrics (e.g., median vs mean) are calculated; clarify methodology for transparency.
The concluding model in Figure 27 summarizes findings well but would benefit from illustrating potential policy or planning interventions alongside causal factors.
